qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded in-memory subscription map growth enables unauthenticated denial-of-service via memory exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unauthenticated creation of unbounded subscription map entries and concurrent map access crashes. The advisory root cause is a missing return after a 404 in HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdDelete that lets an invalid influenceId path still delete an existing Traffic Influence Subscription.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only final qualifying finding is a separate UDR denial-of-service issue involving unbounded in-memory subscription maps and concurrent map access, not the DELETE influenceData invalid-path missing-return bug that causes unauthorized subscription deletion.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-g9cw-qwhf-24jp describes DELETE /nudr-dr/v2/application-data/influenceData/{influenceId}/{subscriptionId}, where HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdDelete sends a 404 for influenceId != subs-to-notify but continues into ApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdDeleteProcedure.

Finding unbounded_subscription_map_dos reports POST subscription-creation endpoints writing to SubscriptionDataSubscriptions and PolicyDataSubscriptions without bounds, rate limits, or synchronization, leading to memory exhaustion or concurrent map access panic.

The final finding's reported code locations are context maps, subscription creation processors, notification callbacks, and router auth checks; it does not identify the advisory's DELETE handler missing return or unauthorized deletion control flow.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 are not applicable.
